question: Does a font-specific fine-tuned Tesseract LSTM beat stock eng on the glyphive channel, measured on held-out data?

recipe.note: Fixes the exploratory run's failures (uniform-random + --psm 13 + 1200 iters + no held-out CER gate).

results.convergence: training BCER reached 0.000% by iteration 1100 of 6000; best model selected at 1100

verdict: ACCEPT (measured). Font-specific fine-tuning eliminates held-out character error (2.311% -> 0.000%) on the base16c x OCR-B channel. Overturns the earlier exploratory failure, which was caused by the broken recipe (--psm 13, 1200 iters, no held-out gate), not by the approach.
caveats: Single font x alphabet cell (the shipped channel); a full sweep would confirm generality across fonts/degradation.
Ground truth was clean-rendered (degrade_image=false); real-scan robustness of the fine-tune is not measured here and would need the physical_scan_regression_gate fixture.
0.000% on 40 held-out lines is a strong signal but a small eval set; a larger eval set would tighten the bound.
These are VM diagnostic numbers; any shipped accuracy claim must come from CI.
